title: SP-21 Tidbit Scribe: Verify-Before-Assert Behavioral Ledger
canonical_topic: An LLM agent logs every verify-before-assert action to a dedicated append-only JSONL ledger with BRIDLE-rule annotation, creating first-class observability of verification discipline distinct from domain-content Scribes and general-purpose observability logs.
composing_primitives: verify-before-assert ledger (tidbits.jsonl)
BRIDLE-rule annotation per entry
append-only provenance (no post-hoc edits)
log_tidbit MCP tool
verification category taxonomy (file_check/symbol_check/schema_check/etc.)

title: Cost-Slasher Claim Ladder with Self-Auditing Evidence Table
canonical_topic: A cost-reduction marketing claim is co-located with a self-auditing evidence table containing a $/correct column (cost-per-query divided by correctness-rate) that lets readers verify the claim at first scroll without click-through, footnote, or refund-rail, making the table structure the FTC-deception protection.
composing_primitives: three-tier claim ladder (conservative/middle/aggressive)
$/correct ratio column
R10 Eyewitness Benchmark empirical anchor (8 models x 4 vendors x 1200 calls)
explicit rejection of 'guaranteed' language
point-of-contact self-audit primitive

title: Fingerprint-Based Incremental Index Reconciliation for LLM Agent Session Hooks
canonical_topic: An LLM agent reads a three-state freshness report (FRESH/DRIFT/UNKNOWN) at session-start computed from a SHA-256 hash over file-mtimes of a declared corpus, selecting between trusting the index, running an incremental reindex (~3s), or issuing a warning before any corpus-dependent tool call executes.
composing_primitives: three-state freshness vocabulary (FRESH/DRIFT/UNKNOWN)
SHA-256 tree-mtime fingerprint
session-start hook as default integration point
per-file change lists attached to DRIFT report
incremental rebuild (~3s) vs full rebuild (27.5s)

title: Parallel Preload Multilingual Retrieval Architecture
canonical_topic: Each language is a sibling preload file on disk with canonical-fact invariance guarantees (Arabic numerals verbatim, proper nouns source-language with gloss), served via librarian_context(lang=) with mandatory lang_fallback declaration when sibling files are absent, avoiding translation-at-query-time drift.
composing_primitives: sibling preload files (r9v2_base_<lang>.md convention)
canonical-fact invariance (numerals verbatim across locales)
mandatory lang_fallback response field
locale-formatting breach for cross-language consistency
cross-language parity benchmark (85% HOT threshold)
tied_receipts: K435 Phase C: ES-HOT 96.7% / EN-HOT 94.5% / gap +2.2pp

title: Vendor-Neutral AI Companion as Cooperative-Member-Product Bridge
canonical_topic: A self-installing personal retrieval substrate that runs locally, exposes the Scribes Cathedral and Three Fates via MCP to any AI vendor, persists member memory independently of any vendor's platform, and bridges non-MCP vendor products via adapter modules, with free-tier-standalone plus paid-tier-sync bifurcation where neither tier degrades the other.

vendor-neutral MCP-first architecture composing with any AI client
free-tier-standalone + paid-tier-sync bifurcation

title: Scribe Coverage Discovery: Category-Level Lift Analysis as Cathedral Expansion Roadmap
canonical_topic: A category-by-category lift matrix computed from a canonical question bank identifies zero-lift categories as coverage gaps, translating each gap into a specific Scribe-to-add recommendation and quantifying the cooperative-economic flywheel property where membership-driven Scribe creation measurably increases retrieval accuracy over time.

zero-lift-category to specific-Scribe-to-add mapping
pooled-lift trajectory projection
cooperative-economic flywheel (accuracy scales with participation not payroll)

title: The Conductor's Baton: Vendor-Neutral Adaptive Model Router for Member Queries
canonical_topic: A Cathedral-aware query classifier that routes member queries to empirically-best model-vendor pairs via a three-mode toggle (auto/manual/vendor-lock), continuously updates its ranking table via every-Nth-query parallel runs across vendors, and surfaces member-visible cost-savings reports — operationalizing the Cost-Slasher claim dynamically at runtime.

three-mode member toggle (auto-route / manual / vendor-lock)
continuous online benchmark (every Nth query parallel-runs candidates)
BYOK + LB-proxy dual key management
cooperative-economic flywheel via anonymized cross-vendor routing telemetry

title: The Cathedral Effect: Architecture-Earned Recall in AI Systems Through Domain-Indexed Working Memory
canonical_topic: Domain-indexed working memory auto-populated at query time via TF-IDF keyword routing and full Scribe corpus injection produces a measurable, architecture-caused recall lift (not model fine-tuning), demonstrated with +62–80pp HOT improvement in K475/K477/K481 dual-universe benchmarks using Perplexity/Comet under zero-web-prior conditions.
composing_primitives: domain-indexed Scribe corpus (keeper corpus + auto-keyword sidecar)
TF-IDF keyword extraction (exclusivity floor df=1 tf>=2, cap 2000 kw)
deterministic substring keyword-match routing
full corpus injection as context preamble
R10 three-tier HOT/HIT/MISS grading rubric

title: Personal Discipline Enforcement Layer (Client-Side Substrate Consultation Gating)
canonical_topic: A client-side architectural primitive that intercepts AI interactions at a layer external to the AI model and structurally blocks them unless a user-defined substrate-consultation rule has been satisfied within a configurable freshness window, with configurable failure actions (block/warn/enrich/substitute) and user-controlled portable state storage.

canonical_topic: A recursive scale-invariant federated discipline-enforcement plane where a composable Federated Discipline Cell performs enforce-locally plus federate-upward operations, composing without bound from individual Augur rules through Squadron, Wing, NAF, MAJCOM, Ring, Constellation, and Solar-Scale tiers while preserving lower-tier sovereignty at every level of composition.

title: Year of Jubilee Cathedral Reconciliation Ledger
canonical_topic: Append-only stone-tablet ledger semantics for Cathedral data with epoch-bounded reconciliation events (Year of Jubilee, every N iterations) that produce new canonical baselines while preserving all pre-epoch state in an immutable archive, with IP ledger entries permanently excluded from reconciliation.
composing_primitives: Stone Tablets (immutable entries, identity = content hash)
Caveats/Articles (append-only addenda referencing original by hash)
Year of Jubilee epoch reconciliation (new baseline + Jubilee Tablet)

IP Ledger Exclusion (canonical scope restriction for USPTO filings)

title: User-Controlled Timeline Forking with Full Chronicler-Tape Carry-Forward
canonical_topic: A method enabling users to select moment T in a component-state history, instantiate a new timeline anchored at T carrying the full Chronicler iterative-state tape up-to-T into forked components, while the original timeline continues forward independently and all pre-T history remains queryable by both timelines.

Chronicler tape carry-forward into new timeline
new timeline tablet instantiation (T-fork-<hash>)
original timeline forward-independence preservation
Year of Jubilee pre-T immutable archive queryable by both timelines

"Yes ratified: 'user-controlled timeline forking with full Chronicler-tape carry-forward.'"

title: The Pheromone Substrate (Stigmergic Cross-Scribe Index)
canonical_topic: A stigmergic cross-Scribe index where each Scribe emits topic-tag pheromones to a shared substrate at tablet-write time, enabling constant-time cross-Scribe investigation queries without polling individual Scribes — demonstrated with 14ms index build / <1ms query latency over 12 Scribes / 345 tablets / 4841 topics, providing ~10^6 speedup over Detective RPC sweep.

title: The Amplifier Threshold System
canonical_topic: A participation-ladder mechanism where amplifiers self-classify into six threshold bands (T1=10 to T6=25K rolling-30d hits/post average) via stamp system measurement, earning time-shifted broadcast access rewards through a three-layer standing model (active threshold / banked Joules / high-water floor) that prevents demoralizing drops during life-event gaps.
